data.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

All four acceptance criteria are met by the implementation in `tests/_isolation.py` and `tests/conftest.py`.

### Criterion 1 ✅ — Guard flags writes into pre-existing `.rebar/` (verified by self-tests)

**`tests/_isolation.py` lines 23–38**: `repo_leak_snapshot()` now watches `LEAK_WATCH_SUBDIRS = (".rebar",)` one level deep — it adds `".rebar/<entry>"` to the snapshot set, so a new entry inside a pre-existing `.rebar/` shows up in the before→after diff.

**`tests/conftest.py` lines 158–165**: `_no_repo_root_leaks` uses `repo_leak_snapshot` (instead of raw `os.listdir`), so it inherits the deeper watch.

Two self-tests verify end-to-end:
- `test_leak_snapshot_catches_write_into_preexisting_state_dir` (primitive, line 74) — asserts `.rebar/run_snapshots` appears in the diff when `.rebar/` pre-exists.
- `test_leak_guard_fails_a_test_that_writes_into_preexisting_state_dir` (pytester end-to-end, line 200) — asserts `passed=1, errors=1`, non-zero exit, and `*leaked into REPO_ROOT*.rebar/leaked.txt*` in output, exactly as the ticket requires.

### Criterion 2 ✅ — No false positives from dirs with legitimate churn

Only `.rebar` is in `LEAK_WATCH_SUBDIRS`; `.git/`, `.tickets-tracker/`, `.venv/`, etc. are explicitly not watched (noted in `_isolation.py` lines 20–22). A clean test (`test_guard_passes_an_isolated_test`, line 167) passes.

### Criterion 3 ✅ — Check stays inexpensive

The snapshot does at most one extra `os.listdir` per entry in `LEAK_WATCH_SUBDIRS` (currently one dir), one level deep. No recursive walk of large dirs.

### Criterion 4 ✅ — Leaked deeper paths are cleaned up best-effort

`conftest.py` lines 169–180 sort leaked names deepest-first (`key=len, reverse=True`) so `.rebar/leaked.txt` is cleaned up before (if applicable) its parent. Both files and directories are handled; errors are suppressed so cleanup races don't hide the real failure message.
